title: DIMMER
abstract: A triac dimmer switch has an LC filter between the load output and the incoming AC supply, in order to protect the load against fluctuations in the incoming AC.
claims: Claims 1. A dimmer comprising: a triac having first and second main terminals and a gate terminal; an output having a first terminal connected to the first main terminal of the triac and having a second terminal; an inductor having a first terminal connected to the second main terminal of the triac and having a second terminal, a capacitor connected between the second terminals of the output and of the inductor; connections for supplying AC power to the second terminals of the output and of the inductor; and a control unit for triggering the triac at a controilably variable phase in each half cycle of the AC power, 2. The dimmer according to claim 1, wherein the output is a receptacle into which a removable or exchangeable load can be connected. 3. The dimmer according to claim 1, wherein the control unit comprises an RC circuit with a variable resistor controlling a rate of charge of a capacitor to a threshold set by a bidirectional diode. 4. The dimmer according to claim 1, further comprising a fuse, an on/off switch, and a positive temperature coefficient (PTC) resistor on one of the connections for AC power. 5. The dimmer according to claim 4, wherein the FTC resistor is electrically in circuit between the inductor and the triac. 6. The dimmer according to claim 4, wherein the PTC resistor is physically located sufficiently close to the triac for overheating of the triac to cause heating and an increase in the resistance of the PTC resistor.
